title: Taiwan tracks 7 Chinese ships and 2 military aircraft
summary: Taiwan's Ministry of National Defense tracked two Chinese military aircraft and seven naval ships operating around Taiwan from 6 a.
contents:
# Taiwan tracks 7 Chinese ships and 2 military aircraft

## Overview
Taiwan's Ministry of National Defense tracked two Chinese military aircraft and seven naval ships operating around Taiwan from 6 a.m. on Thursday to the same time on Friday. Neither aircraft crossed the median line or entered Taiwan’s air defense identification zone (ADIZ). Taiwan responded by deploying aircraft, naval ships, and coastal-based missile systems to monitor the activity.

## Reporting Window
- Reported timeframe: 2026-04-24
- Location: Taiwan Strait and around Taiwan

## Key Details
- Two Chinese military aircraft conducted sorties around Taiwan.
- Seven Chinese naval ships got tracked in the area.
- Activity occurred between 6 a.m. Thursday and 6 a.m. Friday.
- No aircraft entered Taiwan’s ADIZ or crossed the median line.
- Taiwan deployed aircraft, ships, and missile systems for monitoring.
- This remains part of ongoing gray zone tactics by China since September 2020.

## Source Context
The @PLATracker account on X (Twitter) covers PLATracker's subject area as documented in this repository's source manifest. The development above appears in news reporting that aligns with the topical scope this account monitors. Verification stems from the cited publication, not from a direct tweet capture (Twitter API access remained blocked during this collection cycle).
